title: Fix Authentication Token Expiry Bug
description: Users are being logged out prematurely. The JWT token expiry is not being handled correctly on the frontend. Investigate the auth flow and implement a fix that properly handles token refresh.
requirements: Identify the root cause of premature logout
Implement token refresh mechanism with axios interceptor
Queue pending API calls during token refresh to prevent race conditions
Add proper error handling for expired refresh tokens
Write a brief summary of the fix with before/after behavior
client_context:
Subject: Urgent - Users getting logged out

Hi Team,

We've had 15+ support tickets in the last 2 days about users being randomly logged out of the dashboard. This is affecting our retention metrics. Can you please look into this ASAP?

- Rohan (Client)
expected_deliverable: Code fix with token refresh interceptor + summary of changes

title: Design User Profile API Endpoint
description: Create a RESTful API endpoint that allows users to view and update their profile information. Include proper validation, error handling, and authorization checks.
requirements: GET /api/users/profile endpoint returning user data
PUT /api/users/profile endpoint for updates
Input validation for all editable fields (name, email, bio)
Authorization check — user can only edit own profile
Return appropriate error messages for invalid requests
client_context:
Subject: Profile Management Feature

We need the profile management feature for the upcoming release. Users should be able to view and edit their name, email, and profile picture. Nothing too complex, but it should be secure.

- Sarah (Product Manager)
expected_deliverable: Node.js/Express route handlers with validation middleware

title: Implement Data Table with Sorting & Filtering
description: Build a reusable React data table component that supports sorting by columns, filtering by text input, and pagination. The data should be fetched from an API endpoint with server-side pagination.
requirements: Reusable React component accepting configurable columns
Column sorting (ascending/descending) with sort indicator
Text search/filter across all columns with debounced input
Server-side pagination (10 items per page)
Loading skeleton state and empty state
Responsive design — horizontal scroll on mobile
client_context:
Subject: Dashboard Data Table

Hey, the admin dashboard needs a proper data table to display user analytics. We need sorting, searching, and pagination. Check out the Figma mockup attached. Make sure it handles 10K+ rows without lag.

- Priya (Team Lead)
expected_deliverable: React component with sorting, filtering, and server-side pagination

title: Write API Integration Tests for Auth Module
description: Write comprehensive integration tests for the user authentication endpoints (login, register, logout, refresh token). Use Jest and Supertest.
requirements: Test successful user registration returns 201 and JWT token
Test registration with duplicate email returns 409 conflict
Test successful login returns user data and token
Test login with wrong credentials returns 401
Test token refresh flow with valid and expired tokens
Test protected routes reject unauthenticated requests
client_context:
Subject: Test Coverage Requirements

Our CI pipeline now requires minimum 80% test coverage. The auth module is currently at 30%. Please bring it up to spec.

- DevOps Team
expected_deliverable: Integration test file with Jest + Supertest covering auth flows

title: Create Email Notification Service
description: Build a microservice that sends transactional emails (welcome email, password reset, notification). Use a queue system (Bull) to handle async email sending with retry logic.
requirements: Email template rendering engine with variable substitution
Bull queue-based async email sending
Welcome email template with user name and getting-started links
Password reset email template with expiring reset link
Notification email template for task updates
Retry logic for failed sends (3 retries with backoff)
client_context:
Subject: Email Service Integration

We're launching next month and still don't have automated emails. New users aren't getting welcome emails. This is a blocker for launch. Check SendGrid docs for API integration.

- Sneha (HR)
expected_deliverable: Email service module with Bull queue processing + email templates

title: Optimize MongoDB Query Performance for Analytics Dashboard
description: The analytics dashboard is loading slowly (>5 seconds). Analyze the existing MongoDB queries, identify bottlenecks, and optimize them with proper indexing and query restructuring.
requirements: Profile slow queries using MongoDB explain() method
Create appropriate compound indexes for frequent query patterns
Restructure aggregation pipeline — $match early, $project only needed fields
Measure before/after performance with query timing
Document the optimizations made with performance comparison
client_context:
Subject: Dashboard Performance Issues

The analytics page is taking forever to load. Clients are complaining. We need this fixed before the demo on Friday.

- Rohan (Client)
expected_deliverable: Optimized queries + index definitions + before/after performance report

title: Build Drag-and-Drop File Upload Component
description: Create a drag-and-drop file upload component in React with preview support for images, upload progress bar, file type validation, and error handling. Files should be uploaded to S3 via a presigned URL flow.
requirements: Drag and drop zone with click-to-browse fallback
File type validation (images: jpg, png, gif; documents: pdf)
Image preview before upload with remove option
Upload progress bar using axios onUploadProgress
Error handling (file too large >5MB, wrong type, upload failure)
Presigned URL flow — request URL from backend, upload directly to S3
client_context:
Subject: File Upload Feature

Users need to be able to upload their profile pictures and documents. Make sure it supports images and PDFs. Max file size: 5MB. Should work on mobile too.

- Sarah (Product Manager)
expected_deliverable: React upload component + presigned URL endpoint on backend

title: Prepare Weekly Progress Report
description: Summarize the week's development progress, including tasks completed, blockers encountered, and planned work for next week. Format as a professional email to the team lead.
requirements: List completed tasks with brief one-line descriptions
Mention any blockers or issues with impact assessment
Outline next week's planned work with priorities
Professional email format with proper salutation and sign-off
client_context:
Team standup reminder: Weekly progress report due every Friday EOD.

- Priya (Team Lead)
expected_deliverable: Professional email report in markdown

title: Implement Rate Limiting Middleware
description: Add rate limiting to the API to prevent abuse. Different rate limits should apply to different endpoints — stricter for auth endpoints, more lenient for read-only endpoints.
requirements: Rate limiting by IP address
Tiered limits: 10/min for login, 100/min for auth routes, 1000/min for general API
Proper HTTP 429 responses with Retry-After header
Configurable limits via environment variables
Redis-backed store for distributed rate limiting across instances
client_context:
Subject: Security Audit Findings

The security audit flagged that our API has no rate limiting. This is a high-priority fix. Please implement before the next deployment. We use Redis in production.

- Security Team
expected_deliverable: Express middleware for rate limiting with Redis store

title: Design Sprint Retrospective Notes
description: You just completed a 2-week sprint. Write a retrospective document covering what went well, what could be improved, and action items for the next sprint.
requirements: Summary of sprint goals and overall outcomes
What went well (at least 3 specific points)
What could be improved (at least 3 specific points)
Action items for next sprint with owners assigned
Professional document format with clear sections
client_context:
Sprint retrospective is scheduled for 3 PM. Come prepared with your thoughts on the sprint.

- Priya (Team Lead)
expected_deliverable: Retrospective document in markdown format

title: Implement Real-Time Chat with Socket.io
description: Add a real-time chat feature to the application. Users should be able to send and receive messages instantly. Include typing indicators, message read status, and chat history persistence.
requirements: Socket.io integration with Express backend and React frontend
Real-time message delivery with room-based channels
Typing indicator (show when user is typing)
Message read receipts (single tick, double tick)
Chat history loading with pagination from MongoDB
Online/offline user status indicators
client_context:
Subject: In-App Chat Feature

Users are requesting an in-app chat feature for team collaboration. We need something like Slack but lightweight. Start with 1-on-1 messaging; group chats can come later.

- Sarah (Product Manager)
expected_deliverable: Real-time chat module with Socket.io, React components, and MongoDB persistence

title: Build Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) System
description: Design and implement a role-based access control system. Support three roles: admin, manager, and user. Admins have full access, managers can manage content, users can only view/update their own data.
requirements: Mongoose schema for roles and permissions
Express middleware that checks user role before allowing access
Admin routes: all CRUD operations on any resource
Manager routes: create/edit but not delete content
User routes: access only own resources
Seed script with default admin account
client_context:
Subject: Access Control Requirements

Enterprise clients are demanding role-based access. We need this before the next quarter. Admin, Manager, and User levels. Each role has different permissions. Document the permission matrix.

- Priya (Team Lead)
expected_deliverable: RBAC middleware + permission matrix documentation + seed script

title: Create API Documentation with Swagger
description: Set up Swagger/OpenAPI documentation for all existing API endpoints. Each endpoint should have proper request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and example payloads.
requirements: Install and configure swagger-jsdoc and swagger-ui-express
Document all existing endpoints with @openapi JSDoc comments
Define request body schemas using JSDoc @typedef
Define response schemas for 200, 201, 400, 401, 404, 500 status codes
Add authentication scheme documentation (Bearer JWT)
client_context:
Subject: API Documentation for Developers

We're onboarding a new mobile team that needs to consume our API. We need Swagger docs ASAP. They need to understand all endpoints, payloads, and auth requirements.

- Sneha (HR)
expected_deliverable: Swagger UI endpoint with documented API and example payloads

title: Debug Memory Leak in Node.js Process
description: The production Node.js server's memory usage increases steadily over 24-48 hours until it crashes with OOMKilled. Analyze heap dumps, identify the leak source, and fix it.
requirements: Take and analyze heap snapshots at different time intervals
Identify objects that accumulate over time and are not garbage collected
Find the source (global variable, closed-over reference, event listener leak, cache without eviction)
Implement the fix with proper cleanup/disposal
Add memory monitoring with Grafana alert at 70% usage
Write a postmortem document explaining root cause and fix
client_context:
Subject: Server Crashing Every 36 Hours

The API server has been restarting every day or two. Users experience 30 seconds of downtime during restarts. Ops team suspects memory leak. Please investigate urgently.

- Rohan (Client)
expected_deliverable: Fix for memory leak + monitoring setup + postmortem document

title: Implement Search Functionality with Elasticsearch
description: Add search functionality to the product catalog. Use Elasticsearch for full-text search with fuzzy matching, faceted filtering, and autocomplete suggestions.
requirements: Set up Elasticsearch instance and index products with mappings
Backfill existing products from MongoDB to Elasticsearch
Implement search API with full-text search, fuzzy matching (fuzziness: AUTO)
Faceted filters by category, price range, rating, brand
Autocomplete suggestions API with completion suggester
Highlight matching text in search results
client_context:
Subject: Product Search Upgrade

Our current search returns irrelevant results and doesn't handle typos. Users are complaining. We need proper search with filters, autocomplete, and spelling correction. Budget approved for Elasticsearch cloud.

- Sarah (Product Manager)
expected_deliverable: Elasticsearch integration + search API + autocomplete endpoint

title: Code Review: Performance Bottlenecks in React Components
description: Review the attached React component code for performance issues. Identify unnecessary re-renders, missing keys, expensive computations, and suggest improvements with before/after examples.
requirements: Identify components that re-render unnecessarily
Find missing or incorrect key props in lists
Spot expensive computations running on every render
Detect missing useMemo/useCallback where appropriate
Provide optimized code snippets for each issue found
client_context:
Subject: Code Review Request - Product Listing Page

I've attached the ProductListing component. It's causing performance issues on the dashboard. Can you review and suggest optimizations? The page has 500+ products and scrolling is laggy.

- Karan (Senior Dev)
expected_deliverable: Code review document with identified issues + optimized code suggestions

title: Build PDF Report Generation System
description: Create a system that generates PDF reports from user data. Users should be able to request a report and receive it via email when ready. Use Puppeteer for HTML-to-PDF conversion.
requirements: HTML template for report with dynamic data injection
Puppeteer-based PDF generation from HTML template
Bull queue for async report generation
Email delivery of generated PDF using SendGrid
Report status endpoint so frontend can show generation progress
client_context:
Subject: Monthly Report Feature

Enterprise clients need PDF reports for their monthly analytics. The report should include charts, tables, and summary metrics. Reports can be requested from the dashboard.

- Rohan (Client)
expected_deliverable: PDF generation service with queue processing + email delivery

title: Implement Dark Mode Toggle
description: Add dark mode support to the React application. Use Tailwind CSS dark mode with a toggle switch in the navbar. Persist the user's theme preference. Ensure all components respect the theme.
requirements: Dark mode toggle button in the top navigation bar
Tailwind CSS dark mode using class strategy
Persist theme preference in localStorage
Apply dark mode instantly without page refresh
All existing components properly styled for dark mode
Smooth transition between light and dark mode
client_context:
Subject: Dark Mode Request

Users have been requesting dark mode for months. Let's implement it. Use Tailwind dark mode. Make sure it works across all pages and components.

- Sarah (Product Manager)
expected_deliverable: Dark mode toggle component with Tailwind CSS integration

title: Write E2E Tests for Checkout Flow
description: Write end-to-end tests using Cypress for the e-commerce checkout flow. Cover the complete flow from cart to payment confirmation, including edge cases like empty cart and payment failure.
requirements: Test adding items to cart and viewing cart page
Test checkout form with valid data leads to payment page
Test empty cart redirects back to products page
Test payment success shows confirmation page
Test payment failure shows error message and retry option
Test coupon code application updates total price
client_context:
Subject: E2E Tests for Checkout

We've had several bugs in checkout that reached production. We need Cypress E2E tests covering the full flow. This is critical for the upcoming sale event.

- DevOps Team
expected_deliverable: Cypress E2E test suite for checkout flow

title: Architecture Decision: Migrate from REST to GraphQL
description: The frontend team is experiencing over-fetching and under-fetching issues with the REST API. Evaluate whether GraphQL is the right solution. Write an Architecture Decision Record (ADR) comparing REST vs GraphQL for our use case.
requirements: Document current REST API pain points with specific examples
Compare REST vs GraphQL for our data fetching patterns
Consider: learning curve, tooling, performance, caching, complexity
Make a clear recommendation with justification
Include migration strategy if GraphQL is recommended
client_context:
Subject: GraphQL Evaluation

I'd like you to evaluate whether we should migrate from REST to GraphQL. We're seeing performance issues with over-fetching on mobile clients. Write up an ADR with your recommendation.

- Priya (Team Lead)
expected_deliverable: Architecture Decision Record (ADR) in markdown with recommendation

title: Set Up Docker Development Environment
description: Create a Docker Compose setup for local development. Include services for the Node.js app, MongoDB, Redis, and Nginx reverse proxy. Ensure hot-reloading works with volume mounts.
requirements: Dockerfile for Node.js app with multi-stage build
Docker Compose with services: app, mongodb, redis, nginx
Volume mounts for hot-reloading in development
Environment variable passing with .env file
Nginx config as reverse proxy to Node.js app
Health check endpoints for each service
client_context:
Subject: Local Dev Environment Standardization

We're getting inconsistent results between developer machines. Some are on Windows, some on Mac. We need Docker Compose so everyone runs the same environment.

- DevOps Team
expected_deliverable: Dockerfile + docker-compose.yml + nginx config + setup instructions

title: Build Webhook Handler for Payment Gateway
description: Implement a webhook endpoint to handle payment gateway events (payment success, failure, refund). Ensure idempotency, signature verification, and proper database updates for each event type.
requirements: POST /api/webhooks/payment endpoint accepting JSON payloads
Signature verification using webhook secret to prevent forgery
Idempotency key handling — same event ID should not process twice
Handle events: payment_intent.succeeded, payment_intent.failed, charge.refunded
Update order status in database for each event type
Log all webhook events for audit trail
client_context:
Subject: Payment Webhook Integration

We're integrating Razorpay for payments. The webhook endpoint is critical — without it, paid orders won't be marked as confirmed. Signature verification is mandatory for security.

- Rohan (Client)
expected_deliverable: Webhook endpoint with signature verification, idempotency, and event handling

title: Implement Notification System (In-App + Email)
description: Build a notification system that sends in-app real-time notifications and email notifications when specific events occur (new message, task assigned, payment received, account update).
requirements: Notification schema in MongoDB with type, message, read status, link
Socket.io for real-time in-app notification delivery
Email notification for important events (async via queue)
Notification preferences per user (which events trigger email vs in-app)
Notification list endpoint with pagination
Mark-as-read and mark-all-as-read endpoints
Unread notification count badge in navbar
client_context:
Subject: Notification System

Users miss important updates because we don't have notifications. We need both in-app notifications (bell icon) and email notifications for critical events. Start with the basic types: new message, task assigned, payment received.

- Sarah (Product Manager)
expected_deliverable: Notification system with Socket.io real-time delivery + email + preference management

title: Database Migration: Add Soft Delete to All Models
description: Implement soft delete functionality across all Mongoose models. Instead of permanently deleting records, set a `deletedAt` field and filter out soft-deleted records in all queries.
requirements: Add deletedAt (Date, default: null) field to all models via base plugin
Create Mongoose plugin that automatically adds soft delete behavior
Modify all find/findOne queries to exclude deleted records
Add option to include deleted records (for admin restore)
Add restore endpoint to undo soft delete
Update existing controllers to use new query patterns
client_context:
Subject: Soft Delete Requirement

We've had cases where users accidentally delete important data and we can't recover it. Implement soft delete so records are hidden but recoverable. This is a security/compliance requirement too.

- Security Team
expected_deliverable: Mongoose plugin for soft delete + updated controllers + restore endpoint

title: Implement CSV Import/Export Feature
description: Build a feature that allows users to import data from CSV files and export data to CSV. Import should include validation with detailed error reporting. Export should support filtered data export.
requirements: CSV upload endpoint with multer and file type validation
CSV parsing with column mapping and validation
Import results with success count and detailed error rows
CSV export endpoint accepting filter parameters
Stream large CSV exports to avoid memory issues
Download generated CSV file with proper Content-Disposition header
client_context:
Subject: CSV Import/Export

Business team needs to bulk import user data from spreadsheets and export analytics data for their reports. This is a weekly request from the operations team.

- Priya (Team Lead)
expected_deliverable: CSV import/export endpoints with validation and streaming

title: Set Up CI/CD Pipeline with GitHub Actions
description: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, tests, builds the application, and deploys to staging on every push. Production deployment should require manual approval.
requirements: Lint job: run ESLint on all changed files
Test job: run Jest unit and integration tests with coverage report
Build job: build frontend and verify Docker build succeeds
Deploy to staging: auto-deploy on push to staging branch
Deploy to production: manual approval gate, deployed on push to main
Slack notification on deploy success/failure
client_context:
Subject: CI/CD Pipeline

We need automated testing and deployment. Manual deploys are error-prone and slow. Set up GitHub Actions with lint → test → build → deploy stages.

- DevOps Team
expected_deliverable: GitHub Actions workflow YAML with staging and production deployment

title: Build Admin Dashboard for User Management
description: Create an admin panel for managing users. Admins should be able to view all users, edit user details, deactivate accounts, assign roles, and see user activity logs.
requirements: Admin-only route with RBAC middleware protection
User list page with search, filter by role/status, pagination
User detail page showing profile, activity log, order history
Edit user modal for updating name, email, role
Deactivate/reactivate user toggle with confirmation dialog
Activity log showing user actions with timestamps
client_context:
Subject: Admin Panel for User Management

Support team needs an admin panel to manage users. Currently they have to run database queries directly, which is risky. Build a proper admin interface.

- Sneha (HR)
expected_deliverable: Admin dashboard with user management, activity logs, and role assignment

title: Incident Response: Handle Database Connection Outage
description: MongoDB Atlas experienced a 5-minute outage. When it came back, the Node.js server didn't reconnect properly and required manual restart. Implement automatic reconnection with proper error handling.
requirements: Configure Mongoose connection with auto-reconnect and retry logic
Add connection event handlers for connected, disconnected, reconnected, error
Implement health check endpoint that verifies DB connectivity
Gracefully handle requests during DB outage (return 503 with retry-after header)
Add logging for all connection state changes
Set up alert for prolonged disconnection (>1 min)
client_context:
Subject: Database Outage Postmortem

During last week's MongoDB Atlas outage, our API was down for 30 minutes even though the DB was only down for 5 minutes. The server didn't reconnect. We need automatic reconnection.

- Priya (Team Lead)
expected_deliverable: Robust DB connection handler with auto-reconnect, health checks, and graceful degradation

title: Create Interactive Charts Dashboard Component
description: Build a reusable dashboard chart component using Recharts. Support line charts, bar charts, pie charts, and area charts with interactive tooltips, legends, and responsive sizing.
requirements: Reusable chart wrapper component accepting type, data, and config props
Support line, bar, pie, and area chart types
Interactive tooltips showing data point details on hover
Responsive container that resizes with viewport
Legend toggle to show/hide data series
Download chart as PNG button
Loading skeleton and empty data states
client_context:
Subject: Analytics Dashboard Charts

We need interactive charts for the analytics dashboard. Support for line, bar, and pie charts with tooltips. The charts should be reusable across different dashboard pages.

- Sarah (Product Manager)
expected_deliverable: Reusable chart component library with Recharts + interactive features

title: Implement Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)
description: Add two-factor authentication support using Time-based One-Time Passwords (TOTP). Users can enable 2FA in their security settings. Generate QR code for authenticator app setup.
requirements: Generate TOTP secret and QR code (speakeasy + qrcode libraries)
Enable/disable 2FA toggle in user security settings
QR code display for Google Authenticator / Authy setup
Verify TOTP code during enable and during login
Backup codes (10 codes) for account recovery
Modified login flow: password → OTP verification page if 2FA enabled
client_context:
Subject: 2FA Implementation

Security audit requires two-factor authentication for all user accounts. This is a compliance requirement for our enterprise clients. Priority: High.

- Security Team
expected_deliverable: 2FA module with TOTP generation, QR code, verification, and backup codes
